zh.Howard Castle, Wiggenhall, Norfolk, Engl: 英國諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Howard於大約1260年出生在英國諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得時，他的父親Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas21歲，母親Alice Fitton16歲。他於1300年與Joan de Huntingfield在英國英格蘭諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 瑪麗 馮 維金結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1328年9月22日在英國諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Howard于大约1260年出生在英国諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 彼得时，他的父亲Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas21岁，母亲Alice Fitton16岁。他于1300年与Joan de Huntingfield在英国英格兰諾福爾可維根哈爾 聖 瑪麗 馮 維金结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1328年9月22日在英国諾福爾可易亞斯特 文其享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Howard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the Middle English personal names Huward (also Howard) and Heward, from Old French Huard (itself from ancient Germanic Hugihard, hugi- ‘mind, understanding, spirit’ + hard- ‘hardy, bold’). As Hugh appears in Middle English as both How and Hew, this is the definite origin of Heward and a source of Howard. This surname is also very common among African Americans. See Hugh .

English: from the Middle English personal name Haward or Howard, usually an Anglicized form of Old Danish Hāwarth (Old Norse Hávarthr, from há ‘high’ + varthr ‘guard, guardian, warden’). Alternation between Haward and Howard may have led to later confusion with Hayward .
English: occasionally a variant of   Ewart   2.

variants.name: Fogarty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, James Patrick, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fógartaigh ‘son of Fógartach’, a personal name from fógartha ‘proclaimed, banished, outlawed’. It is sometimes Anglicized as Howard .

variants.name: Huggard
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the medieval personal name Hugard, a variant of Huard (see Howard ). This surname is also established in Ireland (Kerry).

variants.name: Hayward
meanings: occupational name from Middle English hayward ‘keeper of the hedges and enclosures’ (Old English hēgweard), a manorial officer in charge of the common fields who protected corn and hay from cattle, theft, or damage, and supervised the harvest.

variants.name: Huard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Adelard, Jacques, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Marcel, Alcide, Amiee, Armand, Aurele, Clermont, Fernard.
French and Channel Islands (Jersey): from the Old French personal name Huart, either identical in origin with Howard 1 or a pet form of Old French Hue .
History: Jean Huard from Courson-Monteloup in Essonne, France, married Anne-Marie Amiot in Quebec City, QC, in 1670.

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When William Howard was born about 1260, in Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, England, his father, Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas, was 21 and his mother, Alice Fitton, was 16. He married Joan de Huntingfield in 1300, in Wiggenhall St Mary the Virgin, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 22 September 1328, in East Winch, Norfolk, England, at the age of 69.

Il a épousé Joan de Huntingfield en 1300, à Wiggenhall St Mary the Virgin, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 22 septembre 1328, à East Winch, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans.

ja.Howard Castle, Wiggenhall, Norfolk, Engl: イギリスノーフォルクウィグジェンハル スト ペター
ja.lifeSummary: William Howardが約1260年にイギリスノーフォルクウィグジェンハル スト ペターで生まれた時，父Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleasは21歳，母Alice Fittonは16歳であった。1300年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクウィグジェンハル スト マリー ス バージンでJoan de Huntingfieldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1328年9月22日にイギリスノーフォルクエアスト ウィンチで69歳で死去した。

ko.Wiggenhall, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 위겐홀 스트 메리 테 버긴
ko.lifeSummary: William Howard(이)가 대략 1260년 영국 노르폴크 위겐홀 스트 피터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Fitton(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1300년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 위겐홀 스트 메리 테 버긴에서 Joan de Huntingfield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1328년 9월 22일 영국 노르폴크 이스트 윈치에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Howard nasceu aproximadamente 1260, em Wiggenhall St Peter, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Alice Fitton, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Huntingfield em 1300, em Wiggenhall St Mary the Virgin, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. ru.Wiggenhall, Norfolk, England: вигенолл ст мэри т виргин, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Howard родился около 1260 г. в местности вигенолл ст питир, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Howard Justice Of Common Pleas было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Alice Fitton, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Huntingfield в 1300 г. в местности вигенолл ст мэри т виргин, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 22 сентября 1328 г. в местности эаст винч, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 69 лет.
